pr.title: Add Windows post-#151 revalidation runbook (lane-2 8-point gate)
pr.description:
## Summary

Phase-1a docs: a dedicated runbook operationalizing the **8-point post-#151 Windows revalidation** — the lane-2 gate that converts Windows evidence from *attested* to *captured* for the execution-chain / evidence-promotion paths. Until now this checklist lived only in a knowledge note and chat; this makes it a repeatable, source-linked runbook under `docs/runbooks/`.

The runbook is deliberately thin: it references the existing `windows-revit-build-test-runbook.md` and `local-runner-runbook.md` for environment/build detail instead of duplicating them, and adds only the revalidation-specific procedure:

1. Local Runner `git_status`
2. Local Runner `ruff`
3. `execution-chain-run` (via the PR #50 runner action) → `artifacts\execution_chain\<run>\evidence.json`
4. `capability-evidence-apply` (via the PR #50 runner action) → confidence/intake records
5. Targeted tests (`test_local_runner`, `test_command_registry`, `test_execution_chain_orchestrator`, `test_evidence_promotion`)
6. CLI Validation Evidence Recorder (if in scope)
7. No tracked source files modified
8. Only gitignored artifact folders produced

Includes the pre-#151 context (`Resolved path escapes artifacts root: '<uuid>'`, 38/77/1 failure cluster), a required success status line, and a failure path that routes into `docs/logs/bug-validation-log.md` rather than a chat note.

## Dependency
Steps 3–4 use the `execution_chain_run` / `capability_evidence_apply` Local Runner actions added in **PR #50**. The runbook states `main` must be synced at/past #151 **and** #50 before running.

## What did NOT change
- Docs only. No code, no runtime behavior, no CLI, no tests. Does not itself run the revalidation — it is the operator procedure (Plamen executes on `C:\Dev\Axiom\Code\Axiom-platform`).

## Tests run
- None — docs-only change (per tiered-testing policy). No Revit live validation required; 2024 baseline unaffected.

## Factory relevance
Strengthens evidence quality: gives lane-2 a repeatable, captured-evidence gate so the substrate loop can be trusted on the real Windows target before Revit-live work.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Add Windows post-#151 revalidation runbook (lane-2 8-point gate)

Operationalizes the 8-point post-#151 Windows revalidation: Local Runner git_status/ruff, execution-chain-run + capability-evidence-apply (PR #50 actions), targeted chain/promotion tests, no-tracked-source-change and gitignored-artifacts checks. Turns lane-2 evidence from attested to captured. Docs only.

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
